'''David Fa'alogo''' (born 9 September 1980) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} and {{rlp|lk}} in the 2000s and 2010s. He played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Ipswich Jets]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He represented both [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at international level.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, Fa'alogo is of [[Samoan people|Samoan]] descent. He is the younger brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Sala Fa'alogo]].

Fa'alogo played his junior football for the [[Mount Albert Lions]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]], and Papatoetoe Panthers before being signed by the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref name=coffey>Coffey and Wood ''The Kiwis: 100 Years of International Rugby League'' {{ISBN|1-86971-090-8}}</ref>

==Professional playing career==
===South Sydney Rabbitohs===
In Round 10 of [[2003 NRL season]] Fa'alogo made his [[NRL]] début for the Rabbitohs against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/f/faalogo_david/index.php|title=David Fa'alogo at rleague.com|access-date=9 September 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121011120000/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/f/faalogo_david/index.php|archive-date=11 October 2012}}</ref>
In 2006, Fa'alogo made his international début for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[ANZAC Test]]. He played in 4 more games at the end of the season.
In 2006, he won the [[George Piggins Medal]] as South Sydney's best and fairest player of the season.<ref>{{cite web
|title=Club records 
|work=rabbitohs.com.au 
|publisher= [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] 
|year=2007 
|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/The-Club/Tradition/Club-Records.html 
|access-date=30 November 2009 
|url-status=dead 
|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120319204248/http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/The-Club/Tradition/Club-Records.html 
|archive-date=19 March 2012 
}}</ref>

Fa'alogo was selected to play for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national team]] at lock forward in the 2007 [[ANZAC Test]] loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Anzac Test match 2007|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/anzac-test-match-2007/australia-vs-new-zealand.html|work=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|access-date=1 September 2013}}</ref> In Round 25 of the [[2007 NRL season]], Fa'alogo was sin binned against the [[Sydney Roosters]] after punching Roosters player [[Braith Anasta]], which resulted in a seven-week suspension from the [[NRL Judiciary]].

In 2008, he was sent home from a club trip to [[Florida]], United States, and fined $10,000 after punching a member of the public at a taxi rank.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/2008/02/04/souths-fine-faalogo-for-florida-fight/|title=Souths fine Fa'alogo for Florida fight|publisher=The Roar|access-date=1 March 2016}}</ref>
[[File:David Fa'alogo.jpg|right|thumb|Fa'alogo playing for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in 2008]]
Fa'alogo was again selected for the ANZAC Test again in 2008. In August 2008, Fa'alogo was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=8 August 2008|access-date=8 August 2008}}</ref> and in October 2008, he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#New Zealand|Kiwi]] squad.<ref name="Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656238.stm|title=Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref> Fa'alogo played in the New Zealand team that defeated [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the final of the 2008 World Cup to claim New Zealand's first title.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7736947.stm|title=Australia 20–34 New Zealand|publisher=news.bbc.co.uk (BBC Sport)|access-date=14 May 2009| date=22 November 2008}}</ref>

In September 2009, he punched coach [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]] during end of season celebrations, knocking him to the ground. He was reportedly provoked by Taylor's 'rough horseplay'<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,26045810-5018866,00.html|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=Fox Sports|access-date=1 March 2016}}</ref> and had his contract terminated due to the incident.

===Huddersfield Giants===
In May 2009, Fa'alogo signed a four-year contract with [[Super League]] club [[Huddersfield Giants]] starting in 2010. In 2011, Fa'alogo was selected in the inaugural [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the [[International Origin]] match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}}</ref> He was again selected to play for the Exiles in 2012.

===Newcastle Knights===
On 3 October 2012, Fa'alogo signed a one-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights%20sign%20David%20Fa%E2%80%99alogo/64776|title=Knights sign David Fa'alogo|access-date=3 October 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131019122807/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights%20sign%20David%20Fa%E2%80%99alogo/64776|archive-date=19 October 2013}}</ref>

On 22 May 2013, Fa'alogo re-signed with the Newcastle club on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/David-Faalogo-resigns-for-2014/75287|title=David Fa'alogo re-signs for 2014|access-date=23 May 2013|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130614082414/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/David-Faalogo-resigns-for-2014/75287|archive-date=14 June 2013}}</ref>

In October 2013, Fa'alogo was selected for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign. He featured in all four of the Samoans' games at the Tournament.

In May 2014, Fa'alogo captained [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the 2014 Pacific Rugby League International. He led [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] to a 32–16 win and a place in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]].

On 8 September 2014, Fa'alogo was selected for the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] Four Nations train-on squad.<ref>{{cite web|author=Zero Tackle|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|title=Samoa train on squad announced|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=8 September 2014|access-date=1 March 2016|archive-date=14 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014081012/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 7 October 2014, Fa'alogo was selected in the [[Samoa national rugby league team]] Final 24-man squad for the 2014 Four Nations series.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82434/default.aspx|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|date=7 October 2014 |publisher=NRL.com|access-date=1 March 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Zero Tackle|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=7 October 2014|access-date=1 March 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203031215/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|archive-date=3 February 2016}}</ref>

On 31 October 2014, Fa'alogo re-signed with the Knights on a 1-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/10/31/knights_sign_trio.html|title=Knights sign trio – Knights|date=31 October 2014 |publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=1 March 2016}}</ref>

On 8 August 2015, Fa'alogo announced his immediate retirement from rugby league due to a neck injury. He finished having played 198 games in the NRL.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/08/08/fa_alogo_announces_n.html|title=Fa'alogo announces NRL retirement – Knights|date=8 August 2015 |publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=1 March 2016}}</ref> However, in 2016, he extended his career by joining the [[Ipswich Jets]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.qt.com.au/news/more-than-a-footy-club/2911314/|title=Fa'alogo to lift Islander youth in Ipswich | Queensland Times|publisher=Qt.com.au|date=27 January 2016|access-date=1 March 2016}}</ref>
